page: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is comprised of a 2560 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific memory operations and single-core tasks. The stress-ng single-core CPU test ranks in the top 25%, while multi-core performance falls in the middle 50%. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with large-block uncached write bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier and large-block read bandwidth scoring in the top 25%. Passmark memory benchmarks also place in the top 25%, whereas database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput perform within the average middle 50% range.

The primary architectural tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained, high-compute multi-threaded demands. However, this shared model provides a highly cost-effective profile for workloads requiring substantial memory and storage density without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. The combination of 128.0 GB of RAM and 2560 GB of NVMe SSD storage offers high resource density for data-heavy applications.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast NVMe storage, this server is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers such as Redis, and large-scale web serving. It is also appropriate for general-purpose workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and large local storage capacities but do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance.
description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 16 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 2560 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers strong memory performance, with large-block uncached write bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier and large-block read bandwidth in the top 25%. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained multi-core compute, it offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-dense and storage-heavy workloads. This server is highly suitable for databases, caching systems like Redis, and general-purpose applications requiring substantial memory and fast local storage.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 2560 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-2560s-amd features 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 2560 GB NVMe storage, ideal for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and high-capacity NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM
2560 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory write bandwidth and strong single-core performance
Cost-effective shared CPU model with high resource density
Optimized for databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy applications
